content.full_text:
THE REGENTS 
THE 
UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA 
OFfiCE OF THE GENERAL COUNSEL 
590 University Hall 
August 25, 1977 
2200 University Avenue 
Berkeley, California 94720 
(415) 642-2822 
Michael J. Baker, Esq. 
Chief, Legal Division 
State of California 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Re: 
Conflict of Interest Code --
Draft Regulation Pertaining to 
Academic Freedom 
Dear Mr. Baker: 
DONALD l. REIDHAAR 
General Counsel 
JAMES E. HOLST 
Chief Associate Counsel 
ASSOCi A IE COUNSEl 
Milton H. Gordon 
George l. Marchand 
Aletha R. Titmus 
ASSISTANT COUNSEl 
Romulus B. Portwood 
Karl E. Droese, Jr. 
David A. Dorinson 
Glenn R. Woods 
John r. lundberg 
Gary Morrison 
Joseph L Cowan, Jr. 
Philip E. Spiekerman 
Allen B. Wagner 
James Richard, Jr. 
Kate K. Alderman 
Alice I. Youmans 
Susan Amateau 
lawrence B. Garcia 
I appreciate your letter of August 22, 1977, 
transmitting proposed Regulation 18705 pertaining to 
academic decisions. 
Alternative I appears to me to be 
quite straightforward. 
I am not at all certain, however, 
that I understand the intended scope and effect of 
alternatives II and III. 
If you could provide more 
definite information as to the intent of them, I am 
certain it would be helpful in developing the University's 
comments. 
Sincerely, 
cc: 
President Saxon 
Vice President Swain 
Vice President Kleingartner 
Special Assistant to the President Copeland 
Professor Fretter 
Chairman, Academic Council 
Professor Galbraith 
Chairman Designate, Academic Council

sections.question_synthetic: The University of California General Counsel seeks clarification on the scope and intent of proposed FPPC Regulation 18705, particularly regarding the meaning and application of Alternatives II and III relating to academic decisions and conflict of interest.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence to FPPC, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No formal question or conclusion sections are present. Document is a request for clarification from UC General Counsel regarding proposed regulation alternatives.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The University of California General Counsel seeks clarification on the scope and intent of proposed FPPC Regulation 18705, particularly regarding the meaning and application of Alternatives II and III relating to academic decisions and conflict of interest.
embedding.first_500_words: THE REGENTS THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA OFfiCE OF THE GENERAL COUNSEL 590 University Hall August 25, 1977 2200 University Avenue Berkeley, California 94720 (415) 642-2822 Michael J. Baker, Esq. Chief, Legal Division State of California Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Re: Conflict of Interest Code -- Draft Regulation Pertaining to Academic Freedom Dear Mr. Baker: DONALD l. REIDHAAR General Counsel JAMES E. HOLST Chief Associate Counsel ASSOCi A IE COUNSEl Milton H. Gordon George l. Marchand Aletha R. Titmus ASSISTANT COUNSEl Romulus B. Portwood Karl E. Droese, Jr. David A. Dorinson Glenn R. Woods John r. lundberg Gary Morrison Joseph L Cowan, Jr. Philip E. Spiekerman Allen B. Wagner James Richard, Jr. Kate K. Alderman Alice I. Youmans Susan Amateau lawrence B. Garcia I appreciate your letter of August 22, 1977, transmitting proposed Regulation 18705 pertaining to academic decisions. Alternative I appears to me to be quite straightforward. I am not at all certain, however, that I understand the intended scope and effect of alternatives II and III. If you could provide more definite information as to the intent of them, I am certain it would be helpful in developing the University's comments. Sincerely, cc: President Saxon Vice President Swain Vice President Kleingartner Special Assistant to the President Copeland Professor Fretter Chairman, Academic Council Professor Galbraith Chairman Designate, Academic Council
embedding.summary: This is a correspondence from the University of California General Counsel to the FPPC Chief of Legal Division requesting clarification on proposed Regulation 18705 pertaining to academic decisions and conflict of interest, noting that while Alternative I is clear, Alternatives II and III require further explanation.
